evals.prompt: acabei de implementar o OrderService com testes, roda mutation testing pra ver se os testes tão realmente pegando os bugs
evals.expected_output: Agent configures PITest if absent, runs it, parses survived mutants in OrderService, writes targeted tests for boundaries/arithmetic/returns that survived, re-runs, reports final mutation score

assertions.text: Agent detected build tool (Maven or Gradle) before configuring PITest
Agent added pitest plugin to pom.xml or build.gradle (without overwriting existing build config)
PITest configuration excludes integration tests via excludedTestClasses (*IT, *IntegrationTest pattern)
PITest configuration sets avoidCallsTo for slf4j/log4j/java.util.logging
Agent ran mvn pitest:mutationCoverage (or ./gradlew pitest) and waited for completion
Agent parsed mutations.xml (used scripts/parse_mutations.py or equivalent XML parsing)
Agent reported test_strength and mutation_coverage as separate metrics
Agent wrote at least one new test targeting a SURVIVED mutant with a concrete assertion (not assertNotNull alone)
Agent re-ran PITest after writing tests to verify improvement
Final test_strength reported is >= 0.80 OR remaining survivors are documented as equivalent mutants

evals.prompt: our PaymentValidator has 100% line coverage but I don't trust the tests — can you harden them with mutation testing?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs PITest (not just coverage), finds survived mutants despite 100% line coverage, writes missing boundary/assertion tests, re-runs to verify improvement in test strength

assertions.text: Agent explained that line coverage and mutation testing measure different things (coverage vs. assertion strength)
Agent ran PITest and identified SURVIVED mutants in PaymentValidator despite 100% line coverage
Agent wrote tests targeting specific mutators (ConditionalsBoundary, NegateConditionals, Returns, Math, etc.)
Each new test has a descriptive name reflecting the boundary/condition it checks
Agent re-ran PITest and reported the test_strength delta

evals.prompt: preciso que o mutation score do módulo de pricing chegue a pelo menos 80% antes de eu fazer o deploy
evals.expected_output: Agent detects build tool, configures PITest targeting the pricing module, runs it, iterates writing tests until test_strength >= 0.80, then reports final metrics

assertions.text: Agent scoped targetClasses to the pricing module/package (not the whole repo)
Agent looped: run PITest -> analyze survivors -> write tests -> re-run, until test_strength >= 0.80
If equivalent mutants prevent reaching 0.80, agent documented them in test comments and stopped
Agent reported final test_strength and mutation_coverage with a delta from baseline

evals.prompt: pitest keeps saying 'tests did not pass without mutation' — help me fix it
evals.expected_output: Agent diagnoses the issue: checks for failing unit tests, checks for integration tests not excluded from PITest config, fixes the config or failing tests, re-runs successfully

assertions.text: Agent first ran the unit test suite standalone (mvn test or gradle test) to verify whether tests pass without PITest
If standalone tests passed, agent investigated config mismatch between Surefire and PITest (excluded tests, Spring context, env vars)
Agent identified and fixed the root cause (not just suppressed the error)
Agent re-ran PITest after the fix and confirmed it produced a valid mutation report
